source: Claims against Malayan Government. Gammon (Malaya) Ltd.__CO273:670:1
filename: Claims against Malayan Government. Gammon (Malaya) Ltd.__CO273:670:1.txt
summary:
- **Core topic/purpose:** Colonial Office handling of **claims for unpaid contract work** by **Messrs. Gammon (Malaya) Ltd.** against **Malayan Government departments** and the **Federated Malay States (F.M.S.) Railways**, and whether/when such claims could be paid from **Malayan Government funds held in the U.K.**  
- **Geography & time period:** Concerns **Malaya/Singapore** (incl. Kedah, Pahang, Perlis; Port Swettenham) during/after the **Japanese occupation and fall of Singapore (15 Feb 1942)**; main correspondence and decisions run **1942–1943** (with later notes into 1945/1947).  
- **Initial policy decision (Sept–Oct 1942):** Colonial Office position (after consultation with Treasury principles) was generally to **refuse immediate payment** for **locally payable, local-currency obligations** and to **defer settlement until Malaya was reoccupied**, citing inability to verify performance/payment from U.K. records and risk of precedent/flood of claims.  
- **Key actors/organizations:** **Colonial Office** officials (notably **Mr. Caine, Mr. Paskin, Mr. Foster Hall, Mr. Clauson**, and references to **Viscount Cranborne/Secretary of State**); **Crown Agents for the Colonies**; **Treasury** and **Treasury Solicitor**; **War Office, Admiralty, Air Ministry**; Malayan officials/engineers who escaped (e.g., **M. Noble**, **J.M. Fordham**, **M.B. Hember**, **R.D. MacIntyre**, **W.M. McCrae**, **J. Boissier**, **A. Wear**, **E.T.S. King**, **Major J. Cruikshank**, **D.G. Mack**, **G.C. Walmsley**); and **Mercantile Bank of India** (bank guarantee).  
- **Shift toward partial settlement (late 1942–1943):** After meetings (notably **1 Dec 1942**) and further legal/policy consideration, the file moves toward **paying only those items sufficiently evidenced as completed and unpaid**, typically **against an undertaking and bank guarantee to refund** if later shown not due; unresolved/uncertain items to be **registered** for post-war determination.  
- **Notable payments/amounts (Straits dollars, paid in sterling equivalent):**  
  - Approved payment **$49,627.38** (items incl. **$4,000** Seaplane Channel dolphins; **$3,096.23** Belfield Bridge drilling; **$42,531.15** Jerantut hutted camp balance) with refund guarantee (undertaking dated **25 Jun 1943**).  
  - Later approved payment for items **5, 6, 7, 10** totaling **$69,069.68** (incl. **10% retention monies** and other Kedah/PWD items), authorised **26 Jul 1943**; Gammon acknowledged receipt in sterling (**£8,058.2.7d**) on **6 Aug 1943**.  
  - For F.M.S. Railways claims, **$10,000** approved as possible unpaid progress payments on **Bill No. 3 (Coastal Wharf, Port Swettenham)** and **$2,153.65** approved for **Perlis hutted camp** (letter **16 Dec 1943**).  
- **Major unresolved proposals/issues:** Large F.M.S. Railways balances (e.g., **Bill No. 3 balance $95,254.71** and **Bill No. 4 $21,250.00**) were largely **not fully validated**; substantial components for **materials on site ($38,177.56)** and **plant lost to enemy action ($42,728.82)** were explicitly treated as **not payable now** and to be **registered** (registration noted as not constituting recognition). There was also ongoing dispute over whether some works were effectively **Service (War Office) liabilities** and whether **reimbursement from Army funds** was due; War Office correspondence cites advances totaling about **£1.4 million** with only about **£5,000** refunded (War Office letter, date unclear in OCR but within the 1943 context).
